title: Matrix
subtitle: Open federated chat protocol — agents log in to a homeserver, list rooms, send messages, react. Plaintext rooms only in v1; E2EE forthcoming.

tos.title: Before you log in to Matrix
tos.body:
Skyline talks to your Matrix homeserver via `m.login.password`.

The server keeps the resulting **access_token + device_id** on disk. Your password is never stored.

- **Plaintext only:** messages send in unencrypted rooms. Encrypted (E2EE) rooms are refused with a clear error.
- **One account per profile:** to rotate creds, delete the session first.
- **Need to revoke?** Delete the Skyline matrix session — it calls `/logout` on the homeserver.
tos.acceptLabel: I understand — Skyline will not encrypt my messages

fields.label: Homeserver URL
fields.help: The URL of your homeserver — usually https://matrix.<domain>.

fields.label: Matrix user ID
fields.help: Full user ID including the @ and the :server.

flow.successHint: Skyline holds the access_token on your behalf. The session survives reboots — systemd reconnects on next start.

setup.tutorial:
1. Enter the URL of your Matrix homeserver (e.g. https://matrix.org)
2. Enter your full Matrix user ID (e.g. @you:matrix.org)
3. Enter your account password — Skyline trades it for an access_token at login time and never stores the password itself
4. After login, agents can list_rooms, send_text, react, and fetch_messages immediately
5. Encrypted rooms are refused server-side until E2EE support ships
